Patch Set 3:

OK, but nothing is using these counters. Do you plan to export them through sysfs or debugfs?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

these need to be accessible in some way, please add a proc file or other method of reading.

Patch Set 3:

(4 comments)

> Do you plan to export them through sysfs or debugfs?

Yes (I already have a patch for it), but there have been some concerns about adding new proc files before, so I am not sure the patch would land.

I am thinking about implementing a throttling mechanism inside the coordinator to prevent more than N/archive, O/restore, P/remove requests to be active at the same time. The N, O, P values would auto-tune themselves (e.g. 45%/45%/10% of max_request).

I am lacking the time to do it right now. Maybe in a few weeks.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Would be great to have this patch... as a first step... just saying... right now Lustre/HSM is not usable when mass archiving

Patch Set 3:

Hi Quentin, could you push your follow on patch? Or add the proc/sysfs/debugfs handing into this change?

parents.subject: LU-5216 hsm: cancel hsm actions running on CT when killed

commit.subject: LU-8626 hsm: count the number of started requests of each type
commit.message:
LU-8626 hsm: count the number of started requests of each type

Add counters in the coordinator to keep track of how many started hsm
requests of each type (ARCHIVE, RESTORE, REMOVE) the coordinator is
managing. There is no counter for CANCEL requests as the coordinator
does not keep track of them like it does for the other requests.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-hsm
Signed-off-by: Quentin Bouget <quentin.bouget@cea.fr>
Change-Id: I0c1e9881f2f6a4f005dfc3545b1b51714eb91b7b
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/28677
Reviewed-by: Henri Doreau <henri.doreau@cea.fr>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Stephan Thiell <sthiell@stanford.edu>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
